Title: The Rauvolfia tetraphylla genome suggests multiple distinct biosynthetic routes for yohimbane monoterpene indole alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR); CAD-like alcohol dehydrogenase family
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream of strictosidine deglycosylation; branch-forming reduction step from strictosidine aglycone/4,21-dehydrogeissoschizine toward yohimbanes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a major contributor to yohimbane synthesis in planta, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Rauwolscine; yohimbine; corynanthine; putative alloyohimbine; minor tetrahydroalstonine and mayumbine side products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes double reduction/cyclization of strictosidine aglycone-derived intermediate, likely 4,21-dehydrogeissoschizine/dienamine, to generate multiple yohimbane isomers; also shows minor heteroyohimbane synthase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of multiple yohimbane MIAs, mainly rauwolscine and yohimbine, with corynanthine and putative alloyohimbine also formed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense metabolites; yohimbine and rauwolscine are α2-adrenergic receptor antagonists, corynanthine is an α1-adrenergic receptor antagonist
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; latex; young leaves; multiple aerial organs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression in almost all analyzed organs; present in leaf latex proteome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High expression across organs; correlated with rauwolscine accumulation, especially in leaf latex
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Manduca sexta herbivory induces MIA metabolism generally, but no direct YOS-specific regulation stated
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: NA; lacks obvious nuclear localization sequence according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast heterologous expression with R. serpentina SGD in strictosidine-producing strain; recombinant protein in vitro assays with strictosidine plus SGD; UPLC-MS/MS comparison to authentic standards
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and assembly, RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA771251), proteomics of latex (PRIDE PXD046315), metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering of yohimbanes in yeast or plants; high activity makes it useful for rauwolscine/yohimbine production platforms
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multifunctionality causes product mixture rather than single isomer specificity; also minor flux to heteroyohimbanes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtYOS (MSTRG.5283) was identified as the first bona fide yohimbane synthase. 2. It produces four yohimbane isomers including rauwolscine, yohimbine and corynanthine, plus a putative alloyohimbine. 3. It is more efficient than other tested enzymes and is proposed to be a major in planta contributor to yohimbane biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produces products at approximately 40/8.5/33.5/18 ratio for rauwolscine/putative alloyohimbine/yohimbine/corynanthine in yeast; considered counterpart of HYS for yohimbanes despite higher similarity to THAS1

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR); CAD-like alcohol dehydrogenase family
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Heteroyohimbane branch with side activity toward yohimbanes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Tetrahydroalstonine; minor mayumbine; low rauwolscine, yohimbine and unknown m/z 355 yohimbane side products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Primarily reduces strictosidine aglycone-derived intermediate to tetrahydroalstonine; also exhibits low NADPH-dependent yohimbane synthase side activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with or partially diverts flux to yohimbane branch through side activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mainly synthesizes tetrahydroalstonine, with minor yohimbane side activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Tetrahydroalstonine; minor rauwolscine and yohimbine side products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression profile suggests contribution in leaves; one of the main contributors to tetrahydroalstonine derivatives in aerial parts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely active in leaves and aerial organs
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: NA; discussion notes lack of obvious NLS for efficient nuclear targeting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast expression assay with SGD; recombinant enzyme assays with strictosidine and SGD; UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering tetrahydroalstonine-rich pathways; caution due to side-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Forms multiple products; likely limited role in yohimbane accumulation because of lower activity and localization constraints
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtTHAS3 is a major tetrahydroalstonine synthase in R. tetraphylla. 2. It also produces mayumbine and low levels of yohimbanes as a side activity. 3. Its yohimbane contribution is lower than YOS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Renamed from MSTRG.5294 based on activity and similarity to C. roseus THAS enzymes

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Tetrahydroalstonine; likely additional unidentified heteroyohimbane and mayumbine-related products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces strictosidine aglycone-derived intermediate to heteroyohimbane products, primarily tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces significant tetrahydroalstonine in yeast assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suggested contributor in leaves based on expression profiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast expression with SGD; metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtTHAS4A synthesizes tetrahydroalstonine in heterologous yeast assays. 2. It belongs to the set of enzymes likely contributing to tetrahydroalstonine-derived metabolites in planta. 3. It shows product promiscuity toward other heteroyohimbanes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two isoform-related THAS4 enzymes named by authors

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Tetrahydroalstonine; traces of mayumbine; unidentified heteroyohimbane
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces strictosidine aglycone-derived intermediate to heteroyohimbanes, chiefly tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces tetrahydroalstonine and traces of other heteroyohimbanes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Likely contributes in leaves according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtTHAS4B is a tetrahydroalstonine-forming MDR identified from candidate screening. 2. It also forms minor mayumbine and another unidentified heteroyohimbane. 3. It likely contributes to leaf heteroyohimbane metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named as THAS4B because it is an isoform-related enzyme

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR); non-CAD-like according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reduction to heteroyohimbane products, primarily tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suggested role in leaf heteroyohimbane metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative heteroyohimbane synthase for pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multiple products; distinct evolutionary origin may complicate predictability
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtTHAS5 is a newly identified tetrahydroalstonine synthase. 2. It also produces minor mayumbine. 3. It demonstrates that non-CAD MDRs can evolve heteroyohimbane synthase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No equivalent previously identified according to authors

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR); high identity to heteroyohimbine synthase HYS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established, but discussion suggests main role in ajmalicine/mayumbine synthesis in aerial parts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces strictosidine aglycone-derived intermediate to a racemic mixture of ajmalicine and mayumbine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ajmalicine and mayumbine biosynthesis, without detectable tetrahydroalstonine production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense metabolites; ajmalicine has antihypertensive pharmaceutical relevance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very high expression in leaf according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not produce tetrahydroalstonine unlike HYS-like enzymes; racemic product mixture
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtAMS synthesizes a racemic mixture of ajmalicine and mayumbine. 2. It is highly similar to HYS but shows a distinct catalytic outcome lacking tetrahydroalstonine formation. 3. Its high leaf expression suggests a major role in aerial ajmalicine/mayumbine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named based on dominant products observed in yeast screening

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Vitrosamine branch with yohimbane side activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces strictosidine aglycone to vitrosamine; also displays low NADPH-dependent yohimbine/yohimbane synthase side activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms vitrosamine and minor target yohimbane side product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mainly synthesizes vitrosamine, with low yohimbine side activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast expression with SGD; recombinant protein assays; UPLC-MS/MS comparison with vitrosamine fragmentation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for vitrosamine production and for understanding promiscuous yohimbane formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low yohimbane side activity and dehydration artifact in yeast complicate product interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtVAS is the R. tetraphylla ortholog of vitrosamine synthase. 2. It mainly produces vitrosamine but also low amounts of yohimbine. 3. This shows SDR enzymes can contribute side activity to yohimbane synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The m/z 353 product seen in yeast likely represents dehydrated vitrosamine

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Geissoschizine branch feeding sarpagan/aspidosperma/iboga pathways and two-enzyme yohimbane route
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Reduction of 4,21-dehydrogeissoschizine to geissoschizine; upstream partner in two-enzyme yohimbane route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; discussed as competitor with YOS for 4,21-dehydrogeissoschizine
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Geissoschizine; in combination with downstream MDRs enables yohimbane products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 4,21-dehydrogeissoschizine to geissoschizine; when combined with specific neighboring MDRs supports a two-enzyme route to yohimbanes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor/intermediate and supplies precursor pool for target product in two-enzyme route
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alone produces geissoschizine, and with MSTRG.5530/5531/5534 enables yohimbane synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Geissoschizine as intermediate; downstream yohimbanes in combined route
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Huge expression in roots according to discussion; coexpressed with neighboring MDRs
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: NA; lacks obvious localization sequence per discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay with SGD and strictosidine; co-incubation assays with neighboring MDRs; UPLC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing alternative yohimbane routes and geissoschizine-derived MIA pathways
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux competition with YOS and other branches; requires partner enzyme for yohimbane output
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtGS was confirmed as geissoschizine synthase in R. tetraphylla. 2. It participates in an alternative two-enzyme yohimbane biosynthetic route with neighboring MDRs. 3. This reveals multiple distinct biosynthetic routes to yohimbanes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog of C. roseus GS; encoded in ADH-rich genomic cluster

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative two-enzyme yohimbane branch with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely geissoschizine or related RtGS-generated reduced intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Yohimbane mixture including rauwolscine, yohimbine, corynanthine, putative alloyohimbine and an additional unknown m/z 355 product when combined with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inactive alone in assay, but in combination with RtGS converts RtGS-generated intermediate(s) into multiple yohimbane products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product in combination with upstream enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to yohimbane biosynthesis only when paired with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Rauwolscine; yohimbine; corynanthine; additional unknown yohimbane-like compound
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme combinatorial assays with RtGS, SGD and strictosidine; UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing alternative multienzyme yohimbane pathways
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No standalone activity; product mixture includes unidentified compounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSTRG.5530 is inactive alone but active with RtGS. 2. The enzyme pair generates multiple yohimbanes. 3. It supports existence of a two-enzyme biosynthetic route in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Encoded near RtGS in ADH-rich cluster on contig 414

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative two-enzyme yohimbane branch with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely geissoschizine or related RtGS-generated intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Yohimbane mixture including rauwolscine, yohimbine, corynanthine, putative alloyohimbine and unknown m/z 355 product when combined with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with RtGS to convert RtGS-generated intermediate(s) into multiple yohimbanes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products in combination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports yohimbane formation only in the presence of RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Rauwolscine; yohimbine; corynanthine; additional unknown yohimbane-like compound
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant combinatorial assays with RtGS and SGD; UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative partner enzyme for engineering mixed yohimbanes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSTRG.5531 participates in a two-enzyme yohimbane synthesis route with RtGS. 2. Alone it is inactive under tested conditions. 3. In combination it yields several yohimbane isomers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Neighbor of RtGS in ADH-rich cluster

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR); homologous to THAS and found in latex proteome
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative two-enzyme yohimbane branch with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely geissoschizine or related RtGS-generated intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Yohimbane mixture including rauwolscine, yohimbine, corynanthine, putative alloyohimbine, and a dominant unknown m/z 355 yohimbane-like product at RT 9.5 min when paired with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inactive alone but in combination with RtGS forms multiple yohimbanes, notably an additional major unknown yohimbane-like compound
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products in combination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to alternative yohimbane route and preferentially generates an additional unknown yohimbane-like product with RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Rauwolscine; yohimbine; corynanthine; unknown yohimbane-like compound
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots inferred for unknown RT 9.5 product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with RtGS across organs; detected in latex proteome as candidate MDR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Latex proteomics; recombinant combinatorial assays with RtGS and SGD; UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq PRJNA771251; latex proteomics PXD046315; metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising for discovery/production of additional yohimbane-like alkaloids beyond standard isomers
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Generates unidentified major product; requires RtGS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSTRG.5534 acts with RtGS in a two-enzyme yohimbane route. 2. The pair produces canonical yohimbanes plus a prominent unknown yohimbane-like alkaloid. 3. This route may be physiologically relevant in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the strongest examples of route diversification reported in the paper
